_meta.purpose: Reusable prompt and schema for generating a lossless project handoff JSON at the end of any STRIKERS research project session. Load this into a new chat and follow the instructions to produce a complete handoff for that project.
_meta.usage: Paste the contents of prompt_for_claude into a new Claude chat at the END of a project session, after all outputs have been built. Claude will generate a populated handoff JSON following the schema defined here.

prompt_for_claude:
At the end of this session, generate a lossless handoff JSON file for this project. The handoff must be self-contained and complete enough that a new Claude chat with no prior context can recreate or adapt any output from this session.

Save the file as STRIKERS_[PROJECTNAME]_HANDOFF_v1.json and also push it to the project GitHub repo so it is always retrievable.

Populate every key in the schema below from the actual content of this session. Do not leave placeholders — if something was not built, mark it explicitly as null or "not built this session" with a note.

The JSON must include these top-level keys:

_meta: document name, version, date, purpose, and an opening_instruction string that tells the next Claude chat exactly what to do first when loading this file.

project_identity: study name and abbreviation, paper type (scoping review / RCT / framework synthesis / cohort etc), authors as a list with Lead and CI labelled by role, full 18-or-N author list, institution primary and secondary, GitHub account, repo name, live URL, survey URL or null, GA Measurement ID.

design_system: full palette as hex values with use-case notes for each, typography (body font, mono font, weights used, use cases for each), spacing system, border radius values, shadow values, mobile breakpoints.

logos_and_brand_assets: every logo file used with its display size in nav and footer, the partner banner file and placement, QR code target URL and generation parameters.

page_architecture: ordered list of every section with id, anchor, nav label, purpose (one sentence), required or optional, and a components list describing every element in that section.

interactive_components: every JS function by name with its exact logic described, drawer open/close pattern, share API pattern (mobile vs desktop), scroll reveal IntersectionObserver config, any other interactive elements.

animations: every CSS keyframe used with its name, from/to values, and the timing/delay applied to each element that uses it.

authors_strip: full author list as it appears on the page, Lead label on first author, CI label on last, institutional line, any project group (e.g. EKAD) in the sub-line.

poster_schema: if posters were built, full content schema for each — title, paper type tag, header colours, layout description, every section with its content, figures used, footer content. If no poster built, null.

github_deployment: repo name, live URL, Pages source branch and path, whether repo is public or private, GA snippet placement, blob API push method steps, token scope.

content_to_replace: explicit numbered list of every element a new chat must swap out to adapt this page for a different study. Be specific — name the exact strings, not just categories.

keep_identical: explicit list of everything that must remain unchanged across all STRIKERS pages — CSS variables, font imports, logo files, partner banner, JS functions, animation keyframes, footer structure, authors strip CSS classes.

standing_rules: the non-negotiable rules that apply to every STRIKERS output, derived from the full session history.

next_steps_outstanding: anything unfinished, deferred, or explicitly noted as a future task during this session.

_meta.opening_instruction: string — tells the next chat what to do first when loading this file

project_identity.paper_type: string — e.g. PRISMA-ScR Scoping Review / Retrospective Cohort / Framework Synthesis
project_identity.author_group: string or null — e.g. EKAD Project Group with member names

palette.project_accent: string — primary accent colour for this specific project, used on borders, tags, buttons, figures

typography.body_use: Section titles (800), hero title (800), body prose (400), lead text (300)
typography.mono_use: All labels, eyebrows, buttons, tags, codes, abstract text, table content, captions, nav links, footer text, drawer headers, authors strip

primary_logo.use: Nav left, footer centre — single combined asset for all pages

partner_banner.placement: Between hero/stats and first content section
partner_banner.label: Collaborative institutions and partners

qr_code.target_url: string — the live page URL, not the survey URL

page_architecture.note: Ordered list of sections. Adapt content, keep structure.

sections.purpose: First impression — study figure dominant, title, subtitle, authors CTA
Key numerical findings at a glance
sections.note: Use for empirical studies with headline stats. Omit for framework papers.
components: grid of stat-block elements (large number + label)

sections.purpose: Compact author list with role labels
components: names in JetBrains Mono with Lead and CI spans
dashed sub-line with institutions and project group

sections.note: Include for framework and scoping review papers. Omit for empirical studies.
components: study column highlighted in accent colour

sections.purpose: Key findings with data tables and stat cards
sections.note: Use for empirical studies. Replace with domains/framework section for synthesis papers.
Use for framework synthesis papers with defined domain structures.

components: numbered concl-item list with concl-num and concl-text

sections.note: Omit if no associated survey (e.g. Hybrid Athlete).
components: secondary group (Share survey / Share page / Email / LinkedIn)

share_api.page_url: Hardcoded to live GitHub Pages URL — never use window.location.href

nav_dropdown.behaviour: Opens full-width panel on mobile, positioned dropdown on desktop (min-width:480px)

fadeUp.use: All hero text elements, sequential delays 0.1s–0.6s, 0.5s ease forwards

orbFloat.use: Hero background orbs, 8-9s ease-in-out infinite, staggered delays

figFloat.use: Hero and card figures, 6-7s ease-in-out infinite, staggered delays between cards

cardIn.use: Hub project cards, 0.6s ease both, delays 0.5s / 0.65s / 0.8s per card

css_classes.authors-names: JetBrains Mono 9.5px 400 — all names separated by ·
css_classes.ci: font-weight:600;color:var(--ink) — applied to Lead (first) and CI (last) author
css_classes.authors-sub: 8px light — institutions and project group, dashed top border

authors_strip.ci_author: Last listed author (A J Hall) — span.ci + span.ci-role '(CI)'
authors_strip.rule: Neither Lead nor CI are bolded. Role is indicated only by font-weight:600 and the role label. No <strong> tags.

poster_schema.note: Populate from session if posters were built. Null if not.
poster_schema.format: A0 portrait — 33.11 x 46.81 inches in pptxgenjs
poster_schema.footer: Sponsor logos strip on white — reuse same banner

github_deployment.repo_must_be_public: Required for GitHub Pages on free plan
github_deployment.make_public: repo Settings → Danger Zone → Change visibility → Make public
github_deployment.ga_measurement_id: string — get from analytics.google.com, replace both G-XXXXXXXXXX instances

blob_api_push_method.why: Files over 1MB exceed GitHub Contents API limit
steps: POST /repos/{owner}/{repo}/git/blobs with base64 content → get new_blob_sha
POST /repos/{owner}/{repo}/git/trees with base_tree + new blob entry → new_tree_sha
POST /repos/{owner}/{repo}/git/commits with message, tree, parent → new_commit_sha

content_to_replace: Hero h1 title and accent span
Hero figure — base64 image or SVG
Authors strip — full name list, Lead name, CI name, institutional sub-line
All abstract section text (Background, Objectives, Design, Setting, Sources, Outcomes, Results, Conclusions)
Poster PDF filename in download href
Results section data, tables, and stat values
Tool arch-card content and download filenames
Survey URL (both in href and in doShare JS)
GitHub repo name in all URLs
Nav section anchor links (adapt to sections present)
keep_identical: Google Fonts CDN link (Inter + JetBrains Mono)
All six animation keyframes (fadeUp, fadeIn, scaleIn, orbFloat, figFloat, cardIn)
doShare() function and share button pattern
Nav structure (logo left, links + hub button right)
Other STRIKERS Research dropdown (update with new project when built)
GA snippet structure (just swap Measurement ID)
Mobile optimisations (min-width:0 on nav-left, overflow-x:auto on tables, -webkit-overflow-scrolling:touch on drawers)
Hub URL in Other STRIKERS Research dropdown: https://andrewhallorthosports.github.io/strikers-elite-sports-group/
standing_rules: No em dashes anywhere — use colons, commas, or parentheses
No AI or synthesis caveats on live pages — belongs in methods text only
No invented citations — every source externally verifiable
No conference or event references on live pages (no UKCCIIS, no IOC Medical Research Centre, no event year) — these belong in methods text or poster only
No bold or highlight on A J Hall in author lists — distinguished by role label (CI) only
First author distinguished by role label (Lead) only — same subtle treatment as CI, no bold
Hospital name: Royal Infirmary of Edinburgh (not Royal Infirmary Edinburgh)
No status badges on hub (no Live / In Development / Coming Soon / No associated survey)
PROTOTYPE label on draft tools (PM-NEAT etc)
Structural and architectural decisions are Andrew's — Claude executes faithfully
PAGE_URL always hardcoded to live GitHub Pages URL — never use window.location.href
QR code on poster points to study landing page URL, not survey URL
QR code on landing page survey section points to survey URL
All pages must include the Other STRIKERS Research nav dropdown linking to hub and all live projects
Authors strip placed after partner banner, before first content section
Google Analytics GA4 snippet placed immediately after <head> tag

next_steps_outstanding.note: Populate from session. List anything unfinished or explicitly deferred.
example_entries: PRISMA flow diagram — exclusion counts needed before build
Evidence strength chart — 18 claims by category
Survey URL and QR — retrieve from prior session before building survey section
Custom domain — purchase and configure when ready
GA Measurement ID — get from analytics.google.com and push to replace G-XXXXXXXXXX
